task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the 'Peppers' sample image (containing red, green, and yellow peppers) opened in an image processing application like ImageJ/Fiji or loaded within a script.

task_completion.description: Configure color thresholding or segmentation parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent using color thresholding tools (e.g., adjusting Hue/Saturation/Brightness sliders in ImageJ) or writing code to filter specific color ranges (like HSV Hue 35-90 for green).

task_completion.description: Successfully isolate the green pepper while excluding others
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence in the application or script output showing the green pepper being actively selected or highlighted, while the red peppers, yellow peppers, and background are explicitly excluded.

task_completion.description: Create a binary mask of the isolated pepper
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the image being converted into a strict binary mask, where the green pepper area is entirely white (pixel value 255) and all other areas are completely black (pixel value 0).

task_completion.description: Save the binary mask to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of a save dialog, terminal command, or script execution confirming the binary mask is saved exactly as 'green_pepper_mask.png' in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used actual image processing techniques
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use systematic segmentation (like color thresholding GUI tools or OpenCV/skimage code) rather than manually painting a white blob over the green pepper using a brush tool.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not copy a pre-made mask
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must generate the mask from the source image. Look for absence of commands like 'wget' or 'cp' used to simply download or move a pre-existing 'green_pepper_mask.png' from an external source.

integrity.description: Verify the correct source image was processed
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being processed must clearly be the standard 'Peppers' test image containing multiple peppers of different colors, not a blank canvas or a different image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires isolating the green pepper from the standard ImageJ 'Peppers' sample image (512x512). The target object is the green pepper (HSV Hue: 35-90). The mask must strictly exclude red peppers (HSV Hue: 0-15, 160-180) and yellow peppers. The final output must be a binary PNG image (0 for background, 255 for green pepper) saved exactly to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/green_pepper_mask.png. A successful mask will visually show a white blob corresponding to the green pepper's location in the original image, with the rest of the image completely black.
